paper_title: Do triangle-free planar graphs have exponentially many 3-colorings?
summary: The source paper (1702.00588) proves in Theorem 1 that Problem 1 (RGEN: existence of \alpha>0 such that every planar triangle-free request graph admits a 3-coloring satisfying an \alpha-fraction of its requests) is equivalent to Thomassen's conjecture (EXP: every planar triangle-free graph on n vertices has at least 2^{\beta n} 3-colorings for some \beta>0). EXP has since been disproved: Dvo\v{r}\'{a}k (arXiv:2108.12669, 2021) constructs planar triangle-free graphs with at most 2^{6n^{0.731}} 3-colorings (sub-exponential), improving on Thomassen's own earlier counterexample to his conjecture. By the equivalence RGEN \Leftrightarrow EXP, no positive real \alpha of the type sought in Problem 1 can exist.

since_posted.title: Flexibility of triangle-free planar graphs
since_posted.authors: Dvo\v{r}\'{a}k, Z., Masa\v{r}\'{i}k, T., Mus\'{i}lek, J., Pangr\'{a}c, O.
since_posted.claim: For a related list-4-coloring setting, proves that every triangle-free planar graph with all lists of size at least 4 admits an L-coloring satisfying a constant fraction of vertex color preferences; does not directly resolve the 3-coloring request graph formulation of Problem 1.

since_posted.title: Triangle-free planar graphs with at most 64^{n^{0.731}} 3-colorings
since_posted.claim: Constructs planar triangle-free graphs with sub-exponentially many 3-colorings, disproving Thomassen's EXP conjecture; since RGEN \Leftrightarrow EXP by Theorem 1 of the source paper, this indirectly disproves Problem 1.

search_queries: planar triangle-free request graph 3-coloring fraction Dvo\u0159\u00e1k Sereni
Thomassen exponential coloring planar triangle-free request graph alpha fraction arxiv
arxiv flexibility triangle-free planar request graph 3-coloring Dvo\u0159\u00e1k
notes: The disproof of Problem 1 is indirect: no paper provides an explicit counterexample to a request graph instance, but the logical chain is rigorous since RGEN \Leftrightarrow EXP is Theorem 1 of the source paper and EXP is disproved by 2108.12669 (and an earlier, unverified counterexample by Thomassen himself). The doi 10.1002/jgt.22634 for 1902.02971 is inferred from the Wiley Online Library URL in search results (direct fetch returned HTTP 402). The flexibility result (1902.02971) represents a positive partial result for a related but strictly different problem.
